title: Still Open
editorialSummary: Famous unsolved problems in mathematics and science grouped by field. Pure mathematics: Riemann Hypothesis, P vs NP, Goldbach's Conjecture, Collatz Conjecture — all open problems in pure math/theoretical CS (P vs NP straddles both but is formally a math problem). Physics: Dark Matter Identity, Quantum Gravity, Turbulence (Navier-Stokes smooth solutions), Matter-Antimatter Asymmetry — foundational open questions in physics. Biology/medicine: Origin of Life, Protein Folding (now partially solved by AlphaFold but the general mechanism is still debated), Consciousness, Ageing Mechanism — open biological questions. Cosmology: Nature of Dark Energy, Shape of the Universe, Hubble Tension, Fermi Paradox. Hard because P vs NP looks computational rather than mathematical; Protein Folding looks chemical; Consciousness looks philosophical; Hubble Tension looks like a physics measurement dispute rather than cosmology.
